revision.comment: Bot: mark 4 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[fa:آداچی یوشیکاگه]][[ja:安達義景]][[ko:아다치 요시카게]][[pt:Adachi Yoshikage]][[uk:Адаті Йосікаґе]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1210|death_date={{death date|1253|6|30}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|occupation={{ill|bushi|qid=Q1975935|1=bushi}}|spouse={{ill|Q106602186|qid=Q106602186|1=Q106602186}}; {{ill|Q106608592|qid=Q106608592|1=Q106608592}}; {{ill|Q106612966|qid=Q106612966|1=Q106612966}}|children={{ill|Adachi Nagakage|qid=Q11451563|1=Adachi Nagakage}}; {{ill|Adachi Yasumori|qid=Q11133367|1=Adachi Yasumori}}; {{ill|Kakusan-ni|qid=Q11630322|1=Kakusan-ni}}; {{ill|Q106644520|qid=Q106644520|1=Q106644520}}; {{ill|Q106684075|qid=Q106684075|1=Q106684075}}; {{ill|Q106768188|qid=Q106768188|1=Q106768188}}; {{ill|Q11451530|qid=Q11451530|1=Adachi Tokimori}}; {{ill|Q11451567|qid=Q11451567|1=Adachi Yorikage}}; {{ill|Q11451577|qid=Q11451577|1=Adachi Akimori}}|father={{ill|Adachi Kagemori|qid=Q2823876|1=Adachi Kagemori}}|relatives={{ill|Matsushita-zenni|qid=Q11528974|1=Matsushita-zenni}} | name = Adachi Yoshikage | era = {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} (mid-period) | birth_date = [[Jōgen]] 4 ({{ill|1210|en|1210|qid=Q5250|lt=1210|1=1210}}) | death_date = [[Kenchō]] 5, [[June 3]] ({{ill|1253|en|1253|qid=Q5498|lt=1253|1=1253}}), [[June 30]] | other_names = Tarō ({{ill|Common Name (Japan)|ja|仮名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Common Name (Japan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Common Name (Japan)|1=Common Name (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15888860}}), Shirotarō ({{ill|Courtesy Name|ja|通称|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Courtesy Name|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Courtesy Name|1=sobriquet|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q59577332}}) | titles = [[Akita jōsuke]], [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]], [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] | positions = [[Kamakura shogunate]] {{ill|Hyojoshu|ja|評定衆|1=Hyōjōshū|2=es|3=Hyōjōshū|4=ja|5=評定衆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyojoshu|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōjōshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyojoshu|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōjōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11288036}}, {{ill|Hikitsukeshu|ja|引付頭人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikitsukeshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikitsukeshu|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=引付衆|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=引付衆|1=Hikitsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q73840}} | lord = [[Minamoto no Sanetomo]], [[Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], {{ill|Kujō Yoritsugu|en|Kujō Yoritsugu|qid=Q608193|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsugu|1=Kujō Yoritsugu}}, {{ill|Prince Munetaka|en|Prince Munetaka|qid=Q708897|lt=Munetaka Shinnō|1=Prince Munetaka}} | clan = {{ill|Adachi clan|en|Adachi clan|qid=Q2975197|lt=Adachi|1=Adachi clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Adachi Kagemori|en|Adachi Kagemori|qid=Q2823876|lt=Adachi Kagemori|1=Adachi Kagemori}}; Mother: Daughter of {{ill|Mutō Yorisuke|ja|武藤頼佐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yorisuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō Yorisuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500678}} | siblings = {{ill|Matsushita Zenni|ja|松下禅尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Zenni|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Zenni|1=Matsushita-zenni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528974}}, Yoshikage | spouse = First wife: Daughter of {{ill|Hōjō Tokifusa|en|Hōjō Tokifusa|qid=Q2225396|lt=Hōjō Tokifusa|1=Hōjō Tokifusa}}
Daughters of {{ill|Ogasawara Tokinaga|ja|小笠原時長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Tokinaga|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Tokinaga|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=伴野時長|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=伴野時長|1=Tomono Tokinaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10856529}} and {{ill|Asukai Masatsune|en|Asukai Masatsune|qid=Q535920|lt=Asukai Masatsune|1=Asukai Masatsune}} | children = {{ill|Adachi Yorikage|ja|安達頼景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yorikage|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yorikage|1=Adachi Yorikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451567}}, {{ill|Adachi Kagemura|ja|安達景村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Kagemura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Kagemura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500682}}, {{ill|Adachi Yasumori|ja|安達泰盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|1=Adachi Yasumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11133367}}, {{ill|Adachi Tokimori|ja|安達時盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Tokimori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Tokimori|1=Adachi Tokimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451530}}, {{ill|Adachi Shigekage|ja|安達重景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Shigekage|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Shigekage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500687}}, {{ill|Adachi Akinari|ja|安達顕盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Akinari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Akinari|1=Adachi Akimori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451577}}, {{ill|Adachi Nagakage|ja|安達長景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Nagakage|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Nagakage|1=Adachi Nagakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451563}}, {{ill|Adachi Tokikage|ja|安達時景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Tokikage|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Tokikage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500691}}, Kōgi, {{ill|Kakusan-ni|en|Kakusan-ni|qid=Q11630322|lt=Kakuzan-ni|1=Kakusan-ni}}, wife of {{ill|Nagai Tokihide|ja|長井時秀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Tokihide|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Tokihide|1=Nagai Tokihide|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11651458}}, wife of {{ill|Utsunomiya Kagezuna|ja|宇都宮景綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Kagezuna|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Kagezuna|1=Kagetsuna Utsunomiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17226850}}, wife of {{ill|Hōjō Tomonao|ja|北条朝直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō Tomonao|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō Tomonao|1=Hōjō Tomonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402136}} }} '''Adachi Yoshikage''' (安達 義景) was a mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} military commander and the heir of {{ill|Adachi Kagemori|en|Adachi Kagemori|qid=Q2823876|lt=Adachi Kagemori|1=Adachi Kagemori}}.Ueda Masaki, Tsuda Hideo, Nagahara Keiji, Fujii Shōichi, Fujiwara Akira, "Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary," 5th edition, Sanseidō, 2009, p. 41. He was a prominent {{ill|Gokenin|en|Gokenin|qid=Q63765|lt=Gokenin|1=Gokenin}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} and the father of {{ill|Adachi Yasumori|ja|安達泰盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|1=Adachi Yasumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11133367}} and {{ill|Kakusan-ni|en|Kakusan-ni|qid=Q11630322|lt=Kakuzan-ni|1=Kakusan-ni}}, who became the consort of {{ill|Hōjō Tokimune|en|Hōjō Tokimune|qid=Q430964|lt=Hōjō Tokimune|1=Hōjō Tokimune}}. == Biography == The character "義" in Yoshikage's name is thought to have been granted to him in {{ill|Gennin|en|Gennin|qid=Q1194430|lt=Gennin|1=Gennin}} (1224) upon the death of {{ill|Hōjō Yoshitoki|en|Hōjō Yoshitoki|qid=Q1028811|lt=Hōjō Yoshitoki|1=Hōjō Yoshitoki}}, the second {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|qid=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. It is likely Yoshikage underwent his {{ill|Genpuku|ja|元服|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpuku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Genpuku|qid=Q138321}} (coming-of-age ceremony) during Yoshitoki's later years.Fukushima Kaneharu, "Adachi Yasumori and the Kamakura Shogunate - The Shimotsuki Incident and Surrounding Events," Yūrin Shinsho, 2006, p. 40; Suzuki Hiromi, "The Adachi Clan," in "The Era of Hōjō Tokimune" (Yagi Bookstore, 2008), p. 330. After Yoshitoki's death, Yoshikage supported successive {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|qid=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}} from {{ill|Hōjō Yasutoki|en|Hōjō Yasutoki|qid=Q1188359|lt=Hōjō Yasutoki|1=Hōjō Yasutoki}} to {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyori|en|Hōjō Tokiyori|qid=Q1354410|lt=Hōjō Tokiyori|1=Hōjō Tokiyori}}. He served as one of the {{ill|Hyojoshu|ja|評定衆|1=Hyōjōshū|2=es|3=Hyōjōshū|4=ja|5=評定衆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyojoshu|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōjōshū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyojoshu|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōjōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11288036}} and was highly valued by the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}}. As a {{ill|Hatamoto|en|Hatamoto|qid=Q572662|lt=Hatamoto|1=Hatamoto}} of the shogunate, Yoshikage inherited the position of {{ill|Akita Jōsuke|ja|秋田城介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Jōsuke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Jōsuke|1=Akita Jōsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11595284}} at the age of 29. In {{ill|Ninji|en|Ninji|qid=Q1194262|lt=Ninji|1=Ninji}} 3 (1242), under orders from {{ill|Hōjō Yasutoki|en|Hōjō Yasutoki|qid=Q1188359|lt=Hōjō Yasutoki|1=Hōjō Yasutoki}}, he led efforts to enthrone {{ill|Emperor Go-Saga|en|Emperor Go-Saga|qid=Q349259|lt=Emperor Go-Saga|1=Emperor Go-Saga}}. Yoshikage played a key role in the {{ill|Miya Uprising|ja|宮騒動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Uprising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Uprising|1=Miya Uprising|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455334}} of [[Kangen]]{{efn|February 1243 to February 1247|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4 (1246), helping {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyori|en|Hōjō Tokiyori|qid=Q1354410|lt=Hōjō Tokiyori|1=Hōjō Tokiyori}} oust anti-{{ill|Tokusō|en|Tokusō|qid=Q1759237|lt=Tokusō|1=Tokusō}} factions like {{ill|Hōjō Mitsutoki|ja|北条光時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō Mitsutoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō Mitsutoki|1=Hōjō Mitsutoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11402082}}. The power struggle between the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}} and the {{ill|Miura clan|en|Miura clan|qid=Q2053762|lt=Miura clan|1=Miura clan}} during this period also reflected Yoshikage's rivalry with {{ill|Miura Yasumura|ja|三浦泰村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miura Yasumura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miura Yasumura|1=Miura Yasumura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11356483}} for influence. In {{ill|Hōji (era)|en|Hōji (era)|qid=Q1327400|lt=Hōji|1=Hōji (era)}} 元年 (1247), Yoshikage, alongside his eldest son {{ill|Adachi Yasumori|ja|安達泰盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Yasumori|1=Adachi Yasumori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11133367}}, led forces to suppress the {{ill|Miura clan|en|Miura clan|qid=Q2053762|lt=Miura clan|1=Miura clan}} in the {{ill|Battle of Hōji|ja|宝治合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Hōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Hōji|1=Battle of Hōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452590}}. Yoshikage maintained a high-Ranking position under Tokiyori's {{ill|Tokusō system|ja|得宗専制体制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusō system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokusō system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135501969|1=Tokusō system}}, and Tokiyori's son, {{ill|Hōjō Tokimune|en|Hōjō Tokimune|qid=Q430964|lt=Hōjō Tokimune|1=Hōjō Tokimune}}, was born in the residence of Yoshikage's sister, {{ill|Matsushita Zenni|ja|松下禅尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Zenni|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushita Zenni|1=Matsushita-zenni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528974}}. Yoshikage's daughter, {{ill|Kakusan-ni|en|Kakusan-ni|qid=Q11630322|lt=Kakuzan-ni|1=Kakusan-ni}}, later married Tokimune. Yoshikage also formed strong alliances with other prominent {{ill|Gokenin|en|Gokenin|qid=Q63765|lt=Gokenin|1=Gokenin}}, including the {{ill|Nagai clan|ja|長井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai clan|1=Nagai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11651463}}, {{ill|Nikaidō clan|en|Nikaidō clan|qid=Q2000952|lt=Nikaidō clan|1=Nikaidō clan}}, and {{ill|Mutō clan|ja|武藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutō clan|1=Mutō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546509}}. In {{ill|Kenchō|en|Kenchō|qid=Q1194270|lt=Kenchō|1=Kenchō}} 5 (1253), Yoshikage retired to a monastic life in May and passed away in June at the age of 44. The position and influence of the Adachi clan, established by Yoshikage and his father, Kagemori, peaked during the time of his son Yasumori under Tokimune's administration. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Adachi clan|en|Adachi clan|qid=Q2975197|lt=Adachi clan|1=Adachi clan}} * [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[:en:Da Liu Ren|Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * {{ill|Sanpaku|en|Sanpaku|qid=Q1815087|lt=Sanpaku|1=Sanpaku}} * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * {{ill|Shinigami|en|Shinigami|qid=Q479033|lt=Shinigami|1=Shinigami}} * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]] == Notes == {{Footnotes|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} * == Footnotes == === Notes === {{NoteFoot|qq=}} === References === {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further reading == * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=1981 |title=日本陰陽道史総説 |trans-title=A Review of the History of Onmyōdō in Japan |url= |language=ja |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=4827310572|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Endō |first=Katsumi |year=1994 |title=近世陰陽道史の研究 |trans-title=Studies in the History of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher={{ill|Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōrai Sha|ja|新人物往来社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Jinbutsu Orai Sha|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501312}} |isbn=4404021569|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Kosaka |first=Shinji |year=2004 |title=安倍晴明撰『占事略決』と陰陽道 |trans-title=Abe no Seimei's "Senji Ryakketsu" and Onmyōdō |url=http://www.kyuko.asia/book/b10889.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher={{ill|Kyuko Shoin|lt=Kyūko Shoin|ja|汲古書院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuko Shoin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyuko Shoin|1=Kyuko Shoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11552315}} |isbn=9784762941672|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Saitō |first=Rei |year=2007 |title=王朝時代の陰陽道 |trans-title=Onmyōdō in the Dynastic Period |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Meicho Kankō Kai |isbn=978-4839003302|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Yamashita |first=Katsuaki |year=1996 |title=平安時代の宗教文化と陰陽道 |trans-title=Religious Culture in the Heian Period and Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Iwata Shoin |isbn=4900697656|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Takahashi |first=Keiya |year=2000 |title=現代・陰陽師入門 |trans-title=Introduction to Modern Onmyōji |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Asahi Sonorama]] |isbn=4257035846|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Nakamura |first=Shōhachi |year=2000 |title=日本陰陽道書の研究 増補版 |trans-title=A Study of the Onmyō Books in Japan; Expanded Edition |url=http://www.kyuko.asia/book/b10888.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Kyūko Shoin |isbn=4257035846|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Ikkei |year=2002 |title=陰陽道 呪術と鬼神の世界 |trans-title=Onmyōdō: The World of Spells and Demons |url=https://bookclub.kodansha.co.jp/product?item=0000195204 |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Kodansha|Kōdansha]] |isbn=9784062582445|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Seimei Shrine |first= |year=2002 |title=安倍晴明公 |trans-title=The Right Honorable Abe no Seimei |url=https://bookclub.kodansha.co.jp/product?item=0000180822 |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Kōdansha |isbn=9784062109833|qq=}} * {{cite book |last1=Hayashi |first1=Jun |last2=Koike |first2=Jun'ichi |year=2002 |title=陰陽道の講義 |trans-title=Lecture on Onmyōdō |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Sagano Shoin |isbn=4782303610|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2005 |title=平安貴族と陰陽師 |trans-title=Heian Nobles and Onmyōji |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b34635.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642079424|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Hayashi |first=Jun |year=2005 |title=近世陰陽道の研究 |trans-title=A Study of Early Modern Onmyōdō |url=http://www.yoshikawa-k.co.jp/book/b32982.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbun Kan |isbn=4642034072|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Shigeta |first=Shin'ichi |year=2006 |title=陰陽師 |trans-title=Onmyōji |url=http://www.chuko.co.jp/ebook/2013/07/514036.html |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=[[Chuokoron-Shinsha|Chūōkōron-Shinsha]] |isbn=4121018443|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 1 古代 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 1: The Ancient Times |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017970|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 2 中世 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 2: The Middle Ages |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017987|qq=}} * {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 3 近世 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 3: The Early Modern Period |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017994|qq=}} * {{cite book
